intro.uz: Unlimited Power — Tony Robbins gʻoyasi: muvaffaqiyat tasodif emas, uning asosida ehtiros (passion) turadi va zoʻrlik hammamizning qoʻlimiz yetadigan joyda. Bu haftaning ikki grammatik mavzusi cheksiz imkoniyat tilida: turli zamonlarda qobiliyat (can / could / be able to / manage to) va xayoliy farazlar (ikkinchi shart — agar … boʻlsa edi). Marco haqidagi POV hikoyada ikkalasini ham eshitasiz.
intro.en: Unlimited Power — Tony Robbins' idea: success is not an accident; passion drives it, and excellence is within the reach of all of us. This week's two grammar topics speak the language of unlimited potential: ability across time (can / could / be able to / manage to) and imagining no limits (the second conditional — unreal present). You'll hear both in the POV story about Marco.

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida hozir, oʻtmish va kelajakdagi qobiliyatingizni toʻxtamasdan ayta olasiz (I can … I couldn't … I'll be able to …) va cheksiz imkoniyat haqidagi xayoliy savolga faraz bilan javob bera olasiz (If I had … I would …).
canDo.en: By the end you can talk about what you can, could and will be able to do without pausing, and answer an imaginary "no limits" question with a hypothetical (If I had … I would …).

grammar.titleUz: Qobiliyat zamonlar boʻylab: can / could / be able to / manage to
grammar.titleEn: Ability across time — can / could / be able to
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 1 & 2 — say what you can, could and will be able to do
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can express ability and inability in the present, past and future
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekchada qobiliyat bitta qoʻshimcha bilan beriladi: gapir-a olaman, gapir-a olardim. Ingliz tili esa zamonga qarab butun soʻzni oʻzgartiradi: can → could → will be able to. Shuning uchun kelasi zamonda “I will can” demang — toʻgʻrisi “I will be able to”.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ In the future I will can speak fluently. ✅ In the future I will be able to speak fluently. can ikkita modal feʼlni yonma-yon qoʻyib boʻlmaydi; kelajak yoki present perfect kerak boʻlsa — be able to.

examples.en: I can speak English, but I couldn't five years ago.
examples.uz: Ingliz tilida gapira olaman, lekin besh yil oldin gapira olmasdim.

examples.en: Could Marco control the stock market? No, he couldn't.
examples.uz: Marco birjani nazorat qila olarmidi? Yoʻq, qila olmasdi.

examples.en: After two months, Marco was able to get a perfect body.
examples.uz: Ikki oydan soʻng Marco mukammal tanaga erisha oldi.

exercises.prompt: One day I ___ watch films without subtitles.
exercises.hintUz: kelasi zamon → “will be able to” (will + can boʻlmaydi)

exercises.prompt: When I was a child, I ___ swim very well.
After a lot of effort, Marco finally ___ get a perfect body.
exercises.hintUz: bir martalik, qiyinchilik bilan erishilgan natija → managed to / was able to (could emas)

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “Now I can … Five years ago I couldn't … One day I will be able to … .”
exercises.hintUz: hozir → can; oʻtmish → could/couldn't; kelajak → will be able to

grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 3 — answer hypothetical "What would you do if…?" questions
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1→B2: I can talk about imaginary present situations (If I had … I would …)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida haqiqiy reja ham, xayoliy faraz ham “agar … boʻlsa” bilan aytiladi, shuning uchun oʻquvchi ikkalasini adashtiradi. Ingliz tili farqni ajratadi: real kelajak → if + present, will; xayoliy holat → if + past, would.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ If I had unlimited time, I will learn three languages. ✅ If I had unlimited time, I would learn three languages. Faraz (if + past) bilan “will” emas, “would” keladi.

examples.en: If you had unlimited time, what would you do?
examples.uz: Agar cheksiz vaqtingiz boʻlsa edi, nima qilardingiz?

examples.en: If I had a lot of money, I would travel the world.
examples.uz: Agar koʻp pulim boʻlsa edi, dunyo boʻylab sayohat qilardim.

examples.en: If I were you, I would learn English with passion.
examples.uz: Sizning oʻrningizda boʻlsam, ingliz tilini ehtiros bilan oʻrganardim.

exercises.prompt: If I ___ more free time, I would read English novels every day.
exercises.hintUz: ikkinchi shartda “if” qismida past simple: had

exercises.prompt: If I had unlimited power, I ___ help my whole city.
If I ___ you, I would start today.
exercises.hintUz: faraz maʼnosidagi “be” → were (barcha shaxslar uchun): If I were you

exercises.promptUz: Xayoliy javob ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “If I had unlimited time and money, I would … .”

vocab.defEn: a random event that depends on luck or chance
vocab.example: Success is not a roll of the dice; it has reasons.

vocab.defEn: possible or easy to get; near enough to obtain
vocab.example: Excellence is within the reach of all of us.

vocab.defEn: to set free or release something that was held back
vocab.example: Unleash the power inside of you.
He decided losing the money was an advantageous event.

vocab.defEn: to share the same trait or quality
vocab.example: What do these successful people have in common? Passion.

vocab.en: to put everything you've got into
vocab.defEn: to give all your energy and effort to something
vocab.example: I put everything I've got into learning English.

vocab.defEn: to develop and grow something gradually
vocab.example: Finally he had time to cultivate his health.

vocab.uz: undamoq, turtki bermoq, harakatga soladigan kuch
vocab.defEn: to push or motivate someone to act
vocab.example: Passion drives them to do, to grow and to be more.

vocab.defEn: to open up and make use of something hidden inside
vocab.example: Passion causes you to tap your true potential.

vocab.defEn: a sudden, big improvement or success
vocab.example: After two months, Marco had a breakthrough.

vocab.defEn: a strong hope or desire to achieve something
vocab.example: His aspiration was to have a perfect body.

englishpod.title: The Office – Asking For A Raise

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda Bill oʻn yillik sadoqatini roʻkach qilib boshligʻidan maosh oshirishni soʻraydi. Bu haftaning “Unlimited Power” gʻoyasiga mos: oʻz qadringizni bilib, imkoniyatingizni oshirishga jurʼat qilish. Sizningcha, u maoshini oshira oladimi — va nima uchun oxiridagi hazil kulgili?
warmup.en: In this dialogue Bill uses ten years of loyalty to ask his boss for a raise. It fits this week's "Unlimited Power" theme: knowing your worth and daring to reach for more. Do you think he'll get the raise — and why is the joke at the end funny?

keyVocab.uz: gapni aylantirmoq, asosiy mavzuga oʻtmay yon-atrofdan gapirmoq
keyVocab.defEn: to avoid the subject by talking about something else
to think about something before making a decision

keyVocab.uz: taklif qilmoq (qabul qilish yoki rad etish imkoni bilan bermoq)
keyVocab.defEn: to give someone the choice of accepting or rejecting something
the quality of being loyal and faithful
to receive a promotion at work
the ability to decide and take the first step on one's own

quiz.q: When was the first driverless car demonstrated on a public road?
quiz.qUz: Birinchi haydovchisiz mashina umumiy yoʻlda qachon namoyish etilgan?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: c) 1920-yillar. Rob 1970-yillar dedi va xato qildi — Neil aytishicha, bu ancha oldin, 1920-yillarda boʻlgan. Tavakkalning bir turi: yangi narsaga jurʼat qilish (risk-seeking).

vocab.defEn: not liking risk; against taking risks

vocab.uz: tavakkalga bardosh beradigan, xavfdan bezovta boʻlmaydigan
vocab.defEn: able to accept an element of risk without worrying
actively looking for and enjoying risk
to make a judgement or decision based on information
to save money somewhere you cannot easily spend it

vocab.uz: nafaqaga chiqish, ishdan boʻshab dam olish davri
vocab.defEn: the time of life when you stop working

funEnglish.title: Unleash Your Unlimited Potential: the power of passion

speakingPrompt.en: Imagine you had unlimited time and money. What would you do, and what could you finally learn or become? Talk about what you can do now, what you couldn't do before, and what you would do with no limits.
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: qobiliyat (I can … / I couldn't … / I'll be able to …) va ikkinchi shart (If I had unlimited time, I would …).
